what_it_does: It stores YAML frontmatter metadata for an image attachment, binding an attachment id to a target image URI inside a pitch deck within the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
No body content appears beyond the frontmatter
standouts: Uses a custom image:// URI scheme to reference a pitch-deck asset
Typed as an image attachment tied to the design-source channel
concerns: The file contains only metadata, so correctness depends on the external image:// target resolving properly
notes: A lightweight metadata stub that registers an image attachment for a design-source pitch-deck asset.
questions: What concrete storage path or resource does image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-17 resolve to?
Does p-17 refer to a slide, page, or specific image variant?
What consumer reads this attachment record and how is it rendered?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file records an image attachment with id, channel, type, target, and timestamp as expected.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a stable, addressable record that links a design-source channel memory entry to a specific pitch-deck image asset.
novelty: The use of a custom image:// URI scheme to target a pitch-deck image asset from a markdown attachment ledger.
contribution: The attachment-to-asset binding (id, channel, attachment_type, target URI, and added timestamp) that a fused system could keep to locate and attribute the referenced pitch-deck image.
summary_for_embedding: A design-source channel attachment file (att-32acc63e.md) of type images that points via image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-17 to a pitch-deck image and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register a specific pitch-deck image asset in the design-source channel memory for later reference or rendering.
